purpose: Determine why rare loaded STM32G0 USB SOF ISR-entry timestamps differ by tens or hundreds of microseconds while steady observations vary by approximately 15 ns RMS.
conclusion: The rare large discrepancy is delayed ISR service, not a physical USB frame-time shift. An SOF edge occasionally arrives while PRIMASK blocks all interrupts, remains pending, and is timestamped when USB_IRQHandler finally executes after the timer or trajectory critical section.
observer_effect: The full attribution build expanded every guarded entry and exit and was removed after the experiment. It perturbed absolute timing and was associated with a post-object timer deadline shutdown, but the elapsed time was sampled before discard bookkeeping and the clear-before/pending-after peripheral classification remains valid.
production_policy: The lightweight guard clears a pending SOF before restoring interrupts, records exact frame and PRIMASK accounting, and retains the already-qualified oscillator map through bounded misses.
